root_text.sn51.26:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu chandasamādhippadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti,
root_text.sn51.26:1.8: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, catunnaṁ iddhipādānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā satta phalā sattānisaṁsā pāṭikaṅkhā.
root_text.sn51.26:2.3: no ce diṭṭheva dhamme paṭikacca aññaṁ ārādheti; atha maraṇakāle aññaṁ ārādheti,
root_text.sn51.26:2.4: no ce diṭṭheva dhamme paṭikacca aññaṁ ārādheti, no ce maraṇakāle aññaṁ ārādheti; atha pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā antarāparinibbāyī hoti, upahaccaparinibbāyī hoti, asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti, sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti, uddhaṁsoto hoti akaniṭṭhagāmī.
root_text.sn51.26:2.5: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, catunnaṁ iddhipādānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā ime satta phalā sattānisaṁsā pāṭikaṅkhā”ti.

translation_text.sn51.26:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four bases of psychic power.
translation_text.sn51.26:1.3: It’s when a mendicant develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to enthusiasm …
translation_text.sn51.26:1.7: These are the four bases of psychic power.
translation_text.sn51.26:1.8: Because of developing and cultivating these four bases of psychic power, seven fruits and benefits can be expected.
translation_text.sn51.26:2.2: They attain enlightenment early on in this very life.
translation_text.sn51.26:2.3: If not, they attain enlightenment at the time of death.
translation_text.sn51.26:2.4: If not, with the ending of the five lower fetters, they’re extinguished between one life and the next … they’re extinguished upon landing … they’re extinguished without extra effort … they’re extinguished with extra effort … they head upstream, going to the Akaniṭṭha realm.
translation_text.sn51.26:2.5: Because of developing and cultivating these four bases of psychic power, these seven fruits and benefits can be expected.”
